data.title: Sustained 20% Growth and Profitability Support Buy Rating on Xometry
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brian Drab has maintained their bullish stance on XMTR stock, giving a Buy rating on February 13.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brian Drab has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors suggesting Xometry can sustain attractive growth and profitability. He expects the company to at least meet fourth-quarter expectations and uphold management’s 2026 target of 20% or more revenue expansion, supported by stronger demand signals in key categories like CNC machining, particularly from aerospace and defense customers.

He also points to macro and market data that reinforce the outlook, including manufacturing indicators returning to expansion and internal pricing studies showing stable marketplace prices with no sign of discounting pressure, which supports margins. In addition, Xometry has already reached full-year profitability with strong incremental EBITDA, and Drab believes the company can continue compounding revenue at roughly 20% annually through the decade, justifying a positive long-term stance on the shares.

According to TipRanks, Drab is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 35.0% and a 76.00% success rate. Drab covers the Industrials sector, focusing on stocks such as Xylem, Xometry, and Donaldson Company.

In another report released on February 13, Craig-Hallum also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $0.00 price target.

data.title: Cushman & Wakefield: Solid Execution, Accelerating EPS Growth, and Attractive Valuation Support Buy Rating
data.description:
William Blair analyst Stephen Sheldon has reiterated their bullish stance on CWK stock, giving a Buy rating on February 8.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stephen Sheldon has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Cushman & Wakefield’s solid execution and outlook. The company modestly exceeded expectations on both revenue and earnings in the latest quarter, and management’s guidance points to sustained mid- to high-single-digit revenue growth with materially faster EPS expansion into 2026.

Sheldon also emphasizes that the business is expected to convert a healthy share of its earnings into free cash flow while maintaining focus on deleveraging and selectively investing for a cyclical recovery. Given this growth profile and the potential for share repurchases, he views the current valuation—based on relatively low multiples of projected 2026 earnings and EBITDA—as leaving meaningful upside for investors.

In another report released on February 8, Wolfe Research also upgraded the stock to a Buy with a $19.00 price target.

data.title: Balanced Outlook Amid Core Leasing Headwinds and Emerging Growth Drivers
data.description:
Jefferies analyst John Hecht has maintained their neutral stance on PRG stock, giving a Hold rating today.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

John Hecht has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, including mixed fourth‑quarter performance and a still‑challenged core leasing business. While adjusted EPS topped expectations and margins benefited from a one‑time gain, the main PROG segment remains under pressure, and management’s FY26 outlook reflects a persistently difficult demand environment that tempers near‑term upside.

At the same time, he sees reasons not to be outright negative, as Four Technologies is delivering strong GMV growth, Purchasing Power broadens the platform into new channels, and e‑commerce penetration is rising. Credit metrics are outperforming, supported by tighter underwriting and better analytics, but the drag from retailer bankruptcies and prior tightening actions, along with cautious risk management, lead him to a balanced view rather than a more aggressive rating change.

According to TipRanks, Hecht is a 5-star analyst with an average return of 14.5% and a 60.04% success rate. Hecht covers the Financial sector, focusing on stocks such as Capital One Financial, American Express, and Enova International.

In another report released today, TipRanks – Anthropic also reiterated a Hold rating on the stock with a $39.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Madrigal Pharmaceuticals: Rezdiffra’s Strong Launch, Expanding Prescriber Adoption, and Advancing Pipeline Underpin Buy Rating
data.description:
Analyst Ritu Baral of TD Cowen maintained a Buy rating on Madrigal Pharmaceuticals, retaining the price target of $644.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ritu Baral has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Rezdiffra’s commercial trajectory and Madrigal’s execution. Rezdiffra’s 4Q25 revenue of $321M modestly exceeded both the firm’s and Street estimates, with over 36,000 patients on therapy, yet still representing a small fraction of the company’s identified addressable population, underscoring substantial remaining penetration potential.

Although management guided to a modest sequential revenue decline in 1Q26 as gross-to-net discounts rise, Baral expects year-over-year growth to persist in 2026 and models Rezdiffra sales around $1.5B for the year, slightly above consensus. She also highlights the breadth of prescriber adoption, favorable access dynamics with first-line positioning and no GLP-1 step edits, and the advancing pipeline, including the planned Phase 1 start for ‘2806 and the Phase 3 F4 outcomes study, as key supports for a constructive long-term outlook.

Baral covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Praxis Precision Medicines, Insmed, and Alnylam Pharma. According to TipRanks, Baral has an average return of 39.7% and a 53.91% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: EPAM Systems: AI-Led Growth and Overdone Pullback Create an Attractive Buy Opportunity
data.description:
Mayank Tandon, an analyst from Needham, reiterated the Buy rating on Epam Systems. The associated price target is $175.00.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Mayank Tandon has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, primarily EPAM’s stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter performance and solid underlying demand trends. The company delivered healthy organic revenue growth on a constant-currency basis, supported by accelerating adoption of its AI-native and AI foundational offerings, which already generated over $105 million in quarterly revenue and are projected to become a major contributor by FY26.

Tandon also notes that while some large AI-related deals are stretching sales cycles, they are paving the way for larger, more strategic engagements later in the year, supporting a constructive medium-term outlook. He believes the sharp share price decline, driven by concerns around the impact of NEORIS’s largest client on near-term growth, is excessive relative to EPAM’s fundamentals and AI opportunity, creating an attractive entry point despite a modest reduction in the price target.
